preFix.title: Cancel one thing this week with kindness
preFix.subtitle: A one-week activity for practising the no
preFix.excerpt: This week's activity: identify one commitment, a plan, an obligation, a yes you gave when you meant maybe, and cancel it with a brief, kind message. Not an emergency. Not a long apology. Just a cancellation.

content.text: A one-week activity. One cancellation, done with care.

Look at your week. Find one commitment that you agreed to when you meant maybe: a plan, a social obligation, a task that belongs to someone else's to-do list, something you said yes to before you thought it through. It doesn't have to be dramatic. It can be small.
Step 2: Write a brief, kind message

Two sentences. The first cancels the thing. The second is warm but not apologetic. No invented emergency. No long explanation. Something like: "I need to pull back on Thursday. I hope it doesn't cause too much disruption, thanks for understanding."

After you send it: notice the discomfort. Notice whether the catastrophe you expected happens or doesn't. Most cancellations are absorbed without incident. The fear of sending is usually larger than the reality of what follows.

initialFindings.message: No orientation paragraph (first paragraph empty or under 12 words)
